title: Tach Guard Verify Result
description: The result of `tach guard verify [--rerun] --json` — the status packet plus, when the verify was refused, machine-actionable repair hints and the exact next move. Read `verified`: it is true only when every required command exited 0 AND no out-of-scope file changed. On refusal, `rejection` names what to fix and `recommended_command` / `next_action` say what to run next. The full command evidence (exit codes, stdout/stderr artifacts) lives in the receipts, not here.

phase.description: Session phase after verify: verified on success, otherwise open.

verified.description: True only when every required command passed AND no out-of-scope file changed.

commands_required.description: How many commands the goal requires to pass.

commands_passed.description: How many required commands passed on this verify.

out_of_scope.description: How many changed files fell outside the goal's fs.write scope (a non-zero count fails verify before any command runs).

receipts.description: How many command receipts this session has minted.

step.description: The session's cumulative durable-effect step counter.

rejection.description: Why the verify was refused, with repair strategies to choose between. Null on a clean/verified run.

message.description: A human-readable summary of the refusal.

violations.description: The offending paths for a scope_violation (empty for other kinds).

path.description: The changed file outside the write scope.

reason.description: Why it is rejected, e.g. "not allowed by fs.write".

allowed.description: The fs.write globs that would have allowed it.

repair_strategies.description: Named strategies, most-preferred first, e.g. revert_out_of_scope_file.

path.description: The file to act on, when the action is file-scoped.

next_action.description: The agent's next move: edit_then_verify | fix_scope_violation | run_verify | finalize | done | aborted | blocked.

recommended_command.description: The exact Tach command to run next (empty when the run is terminal).
